medications.drug_name: broad-spectrum SPF 50+ sunscreen to the depigmented patches (daily)
medications.indication_rationale: Depigmented skin lacks melanin protection and burns easily — daily sun protection is essential.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply daily to affected areas; reapply with sun exposure

medications.drug_name: topical calcineurin inhibitor / phototherapy — via dermatology (elective)
medications.indication_rationale: Specialist repigmentation options discussed electively at routine review.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use slow box-breathing to settle the acute panic; understand this is benign and not an infection.
Apply daily sunscreen to the patches and reduce constant mirror-checking (which drives anxiety).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Protects fragile skin and reduces reassurance-seeking anxiety cycles.

patient_education_points.point: This is vitiligo — your immune system has stopped some skin cells making pigment, leaving white patches. It is completely benign and NOT an infection, cancer or 'flesh-eating' disease.
The white skin burns easily, so use SPF 50+ daily on the patches. We'll check some blood tests (including thyroid) and can discuss treatments to encourage colour to return via a skin specialist.
If you ever feel completely overwhelmed or have thoughts of harming yourself, call 000, go to the emergency department, or call a mental-health crisis line.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Blood-test results (thyroid/autoimmune), psychological support, and dermatology treatment options.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Thoughts of self-harm or overwhelming distress — 000/ED/crisis line; numbness or scaling in the patches — review the diagnosis.

must_include_items: Diagnose benign vitiligo AFTER confirming chalk-white smooth pattern and normal sensation/no scale (exclude infective mimics)
Reassure clearly and support the acute panic; screen self-harm with a mental-health safety-net
Daily sun protection of the depigmented skin
Autoimmune (thyroid) screening and routine dermatology/psychological options
acceptable_alternatives: Referral to a crisis line as the named mental-health contact
errors_of_commission: Labelling as an infection/treating with antifungals/antibiotics
Sending benign vitiligo to ED for 'skin cancer'/'flesh-eating disease'
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires screened reassurance for benign vitiligo, acute panic/self-harm management, sun protection, and autoimmune screening.
